---
title: "Clairvoyant Project, Fall 2025"
author: 
- "Team Joey"
format:
  pdf:
    colorlinks: true
---

## 

```{r Libraries, include=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
```

```{r DataImport}
#df <- read.csv("/Users/aishanipradhan/Downloads/final_data_merged.csv")
df <- read.csv('/Users/louis/Desktop/CMU Grad/Fall/Professional Skills/Clairvoyant/final_data.csv')
```

```{r Aesthetics}
clairvoyant_purple <- "#6957cd"
clairvoyant_palette <- c(
  "#4b3e8f","#6957cd","#402b2a","#4281a3","#c6b9f6","#140f49","#b9cadd"
)
```

```{r Histogram}
ggplot(df, 
       aes(x = industry, y = industry_health_score)) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "bar", fill = clairvoyant_purple, color = "black", width = 1 ) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 5 Industries by Health Score Metric",
    x = "Industry",
    y = "Health Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),                 # removes all grid lines
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

```



```{r RiskReturnScatterplot}

df_labels <- final_data_merged %>%
  group_by(industry) %>%
  summarize(
    industry_vol_12m = mean(industry_vol_12m, na.rm = TRUE),
    industry_return_12m = mean(industry_return_12m, na.rm = TRUE),
    job_postings = mean(job_postings, na.rm = TRUE)
  )



# Calculate averages
avg_vol <- mean(df_labels$industry_vol_12m, na.rm = TRUE)
avg_ret <- mean(df_labels$industry_return_12m, na.rm = TRUE)

ggplot(df_labels, aes(x = industry_vol_12m,
                              y = industry_return_12m,
                              color = industry,
                              size = job_postings)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
  # Add text labels for industries
  geom_text(aes(label = industry),
            vjust = -1,        # vertical position above the point
            size = 3.5,
            fontface = "bold",
            show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = avg_ret, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", size = 0.5,alpha=.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = avg_vol, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", size = 0.5, alpha=.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = clairvoyant_palette) +  # use your custom color palette
  labs(
    title = "Industries in the Top Left Quadrant are the most attractive",
    x = "Volatility",
    y = "Return over S&P",
    size = "Number of Job Postings (Hundred of Thousands)") +
  scale_size_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 9),
    plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 10, face = "italic"),
    legend.position = "none"  # removes the legend entirely
  )+
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(min(df_labels$industry_vol_12m, na.rm = TRUE) - .05,
             max(df_labels$industry_vol_12m, na.rm = TRUE) + .05),  # expands both sides of x
    ylim = c(min(df_labels$industry_return_12m, na.rm = TRUE),
             max(df_labels$industry_return_12m, na.rm = TRUE) + .05) )# expands upper y bound


```
